title: Crew Carwash is a great company to work at!
body: Crew Carwash is a great company to work at!
cons: Like every job, there are a few cons from working here. However, the cons of Crew Carwash doesnât make working here a bad job at all. - One personal con of mine is that when youâre new, there is so much to learn. Therefore, it is a bit overwhelming. Crew Carwash in general puts a bit of pressure on everyone as well, so sometimes you can find yourself being a little stressed. - Another con that I have is the weather. When you apply to work at Crew Carwash, of course you know that you have to work outside. However, it does take some time to get use to it, ESPECIALLY the summer/winter so just be prepared. The good thing is that over time it does get better.

title: Great workplace focused on excellence
pros: - incredibly values based. At Crew we LIVE the values. hiring, coaching, promotions, termination, pay raises are all based on how well employees embody the values - fast growing company. We are expanding a lot and there are promotions and growth opportunities readily available if youâre whiling to out in the work and strive for excellence - feedback culture. Crewâs team member promise is to reach you full potential. All team members, managers, and leadership are coached and given feedback consistently - fun. Itâs not every work place that you get to work with such great people and see the goals being achieved
cons: - being in the service industry there can be some challenging schedules, early mornings, and late nights. - as we rapidly grow there are some growing pains with maintaining top quality management teams at all our locations to provide great customer and team experiences. - as a manager this is not a low stress job, but the rewards are worth it

title: Great culture, Good benefits, Demanding work environment.
body: Great culture, Good benefits, Demanding work environment.
pros: Better than average benefits. Weekend off rotation model (rare to find in any customer facing role). Great PTO (no sick days) Great overall culture, and the training is top notch. Upper management is very visible, and in my first 30 days of employment I had personal conversations with both the CEO Bill Dahm, and the COO Billy Schaming. Great guys that I hold in high esteem.
role: Current employee - Fast Track Operations Manager

title: Climb the Ladder
pros: Clear lath to become a General Manager. Fast track program is great for new candidates. Learn from scratch. Lead a team. Lots of growth.
cons: You will face many challenges getting to GM. Buckle up. Itâs not given to you. Itâs cold out. No perfect solution. Be ready to be cold and sacrifice for your team every day. If you like structure, we have it here. The level after GM is unattainable for many people including myself. No clear path to Area Director. If I ever leave crew. That would be why.

title: Crew Management
pros: I think one of the best things about Crew is the management who has a great mix of fun and professionalism that makes working together a lot easier and effective. Every manager at Crew is not only highly trained but they are also very personable and you often forget you are speaking to a manager! I also really like that every manger you speak to at Crew, can tell you about their whole crew career and how they started out just like everyone else and eventually climbed the ladder. This is great because it limits the disconnect management and employees sometimes have because associates always know that their manager went through the exact same work that they are doing and it also encourages associates to climb the corporate ladder!
cons: The only con I can think about is that our service is quite weather dependent and so some days are incredibly busy and others are incredibly dead which can sometimes impact the amount of hours you work.

title: Working at Crew
pros: - Management team at my location is always very supportive, and helpful. - Having team meetings every period. Although they are really early in the morning, I really appreciate being able to communicate to everyone. They also provide a great time to check in with the team. - The company really does try their hardest to uphold their values. - Work shops, I appreciate how much they want their employees to succeed.
cons: - Not every location has a stable team / management team. I just happened to get really lucky at my location. -Not being able to make friends as a manager. Yes you can hangout with other managers. The main priority is making sure thereâs no favoritism. However that just leads to the managers playing favorites with other managers, and letting them get away with things. - I really wish we had a set schedule. I wouldnât mind if there were criteriaâs we had to hit. Like opening 1 day and also closing 1 day out of the week. One way they try to help with that is getting the schedule out early. However then you have to plan things really far in advance. - Know that time off requests are based on seniority. Also that there are restrictions on when you can take PTO and how much at a time.

title: Engaging work and great people, stretching bandwidth limits
body: Engaging work and great people, stretching bandwidth limits
pros: Thereâs never a dull moment working at Crew. All projects are genuinely interesting and provide plenty of opportunities to learn and take ownership. The team is friendly, approachable, and willing to collaborate when challenges come up. Cross-department teamwork is often what keeps projects moving forward, and thereâs a strong sense of wanting to do the right thing.
cons: The workload can be overwhelming at times, with a steady backlog and limited internal resources to address it quickly. When staffing needs come up, the preference often leans toward suggesting bringing in third-party solutions rather than building up internal teams. While this provides short-term relief, it can make it harder to maintain consistency and internal expertise. Many employees seem to be stretched thin, which can affect quality and turnaround time.

title: Why Crew is an Excellent job, short term and long term!
body: Why Crew is an Excellent job, short term and long term!
pros: - Pay, benefits, and compensation are excellent and competitive for the work you do! - Management genuinely loves their job and goes above and beyond to care for the team! - Upper management is always open to feedback and is very accessible to everyone! - Working with the teams at each store is fun and you build awesome relationships with your coworkers! - There are many opportunities for advancement within the company!
cons: - Crew is a very weather based business and working outside in extreme temperatures can make the job more challenging. - With Crew being weather based, hours and shifts can often be cut due to slow business and it can be tricky to get the hours you are looking for outside of Management - New policies or job changes can sometimes be communicated seemingly last minute leading to big transitions very quickly. These transitions are usually handled very well, but more heads up as much as possible would be nice.

title: Associate to Manager
pros: I started at Crew as my first job in high school two years ago. I started as just an Associate and now I have grown to a Shift Manager. Being at Crew these past two years has shown me that, this is just more than a job. At Crew we work as a family and they care about your growth, not just at your job, but as an individual as well. All of this is because of the culture that we have. This company will pour as much into you as you pour into it. If you are looking for a company that will provide more than just a job for you Crew Carwash is the place to be!
cons: There sometimes can be a big change of pace and the job can sometimes be stressful, but that is where you will see how you react to situations and see yourself grow.
role: Current employee - Part Time Shift Manager

title: Creating Smiles and lifetime connections
pros: There are so many pros to working for Crew! Starting with the benefits. If it wasnât for the tuition reimbursement or partnership with IvyTech, I wouldnât be able to go back to college to achieve my degree! The payroll team, especially, is the biggest pro to working for Crew! They are on time, rarely make mistakes (or take accountability and quickly fix those mistakes), are always available to answer any questions or lend a helping hand! Another pro is the connections you make along the way. During my four years at the company, I have made lifelong friendships! The team members at Crew are the most supportive, helpful, kind, and caring people I have ever met at a job! And lastly, what is probably the biggest pro to being a manager at Crew is the life skills you develop. I had no knowledge/training of maintenance or any mechanical background before I started. But the management track is designed to develop not only those skills, but the confidence to take on new challenges that may arise!
cons: The biggest con is the weather. You really are out there in every single extreme. Eventually you get used to it to a certain degree, but the tan lines are lifelong!

title: Why Crew is the best place to work.

title: Working as a Student
pros: - People, the people make Crew what it is, you will not find better coworkers anywhere else, which is why Iâve been here for over 3 years. - Flexible Scheduling, I was able to move into a Part Time Management position while attending college full time - Great Benefits, even as a part time employee you get a few awesome benefits from the company, including tuition reimbursement for students - Training / Growth, there is always room for advancement at Crew. If you have a goal to move up within the company you will be supported by your leaders and they will help you reach your goals, which tends to come with an increase in compensation - Fun Environment, I can confidently say that I look forward to going into work each day knowing I have a great team waiting for me once I arrive
cons: - Unique Tasks, you will become a jack of all trades working at Crew. It is not uncommon to learn about plumbing, electricity, pneumatics, landscaping, etc. - Long Days, every week you can expect to have early mornings and late nights - Stressful Situations, we are working with customers who are bringing their second largest investment to our facility, their vehicles. If we do not meet their expectations it is not uncommon for the customers to be upset or concerned with the wash - High Expectations, Crew does expect a lot from all of their employees so you must be willing to do whatever it takes to reach those expectations
role: Current employee - Part Time Shift Manager

title: Crew is inâ¦âThe People Buisnessâ
pros: Crew is a fast-paced, team-driven environment with a strong emphasis on fun, which creates an upbeat and positive workplace culture. Leadership is supportive, and there are clear opportunities for growth and advancement. The pay is competitiveâespecially valuable for those just starting out or entering the workforce with little experience. What truly stands out is Crewâs people-first mindset and strong culture of recognition, which deeply aligns with my own values. Highly recommended
cons: I wouldnât necessarily consider these cons, but itâs worth noting that the job can be physically demanding, with outdoor work in both hot and cold weather. There are high standards and a level of accountability that exceeds many other workplaces. That said, these expectations are clearly communicated from the startâand for high performers, they create an environment thatâs both challenging and incredibly rewarding.

title: Purpose-driven company with strong values and real career growth
body: Purpose-driven company with strong values and real career growth
pros: Crew Carwash truly lives their values. Safety, Teamwork, and Integrity arenât just wordsâthey actually show up in how decisions are made, how people are treated, and how success is celebrated. Leadership is highly visible and approachable, and thereâs a genuine culture of appreciation at every level. In my role, Iâve had the chance to grow professionally while contributing to meaningful projects that impact the entire company. The company encourages internal mobility, celebrates wins, and invests in developing people.
cons: With plenty of growth, things move fast and change often. Itâs exciting, but it means you need to be adaptable and proactive.

title: Family Business!
pros: Working at Crew Carwash for over 4 years can be described with a few words - Fun and Rewarding! I enjoy working with my coworkers and giving every customer a great experience. There are customers with clean cars and theyâre washing it just to talk and connect with us. That type of relationship you build with your customers and team members gives me a sense of purpose and meaning. There is daily incentive, monthly contests and rewards, so Iâm always encouraged to achieve my goals. Completing tasks and maintenance items is very rewarding and helps me grow by learning new skills that I can use in my everyday life. Also, there is much growth if you want to move up in the leadership ladder. They are great at giving you goals to work towards while training and teaching you to achieve those goals. As you climb the ladder, the compensation is rewarding as well. Overall, Crew Carwash is a great company to work for because itâs like a family that supports, rewards, and encourages you to be successful.
cons: Working in the harsh environment can be difficult when itâs extremely cold or hot, but your managers and team members are great at making sure everyone is comfortable, safe, and rotated between standing inside to warm up or cool down.

title: From Associates to Manager â A Company that Truly Invests in You
body: From Associates to Manager â A Company that Truly Invests in You
pros: In just under three years at Crew Carwash, Iâve grown from a Wash Associate to an Operations Manager, moving up three levels through consistent mentorship, feedback, and leadership development. I originally joined Crew to learn how to run a small business, and what I gained went far beyond technical knowledge. The management and leadership teams truly prioritize personal growth, helping individuals evolve into high-performing leaders and effective team members. Crewâs culture is one of accountability, support, and genuine investment in people. If youâre driven and eager to learn, this is a company that will pour into you just as much as you pour into the work. If youâre looking for more than just a jobâif you want a career that challenges you, develops you, and believes in youâCrew Carwash is where to start.
cons: The pace of change and high expectations can be intense at timesâbut thatâs also what creates real growth. You wonât stagnate here.

title: More than just a job! Itâs a great place to be.
body: More than just a job! Itâs a great place to be.
pros: - the culture - the people - the work environment - growth opportunities - career opportunities Iâd say there are not many jobs out there where you truly are excited to go in and work. Crew is one of those places! What makes Crew different from most places is the people they hire and the culture that they have. It becomes more than just a job. Iâm extremely fortunate to have seen it replicated on more than one occasion. Transferring from location to location Iâve seen the Culture Crew has built and every time Iâm amazed by how well you can truly have a work family at each store. You have people that genuinely care for you and a leadership team that values every crew member. It becomes contagious in such a positive way. You work with very like minded people and others who want to see you reach your full potential.
cons: Of course there will be cons with any job. Part of those cons to most people will be the following: - Working in weather extremes (brutal winters and extreme cold, hot humid summers) - Fast pace environment (may not be for everyone) - Retail hours (occasional clopens, again not for everybody) - short staffing puts a lot of stress on management

For example, customers would argue pricing and wouldnât be too happy about it. Another example could be the extremely impatient customers on busier days. We are trying our hardest constantly, we are running around in rain, sleet, and snow to make sure all customers are satisfied and SAFE. Unfortunately, some still donât see the value in it but those customers that Iâve built personal relationships with makes it all worth while. I love my customers!

title: Crew Carwash Review
pros: Working at Crew Carwash has been a transformative experience for me. The opportunity to acquire new skills that once seemed insurmountable has truly been empowering. I've grown into a more decisive leader, thanks to the autonomy and responsibility entrusted to me. The brief interactions I have with customers are incredibly rewarding; their positivity and appreciation fuel my enthusiasm and keep my spirits high. Moreover, I cherish the camaraderie with my team. Our collective efforts, especially during challenging times when managers are occupied with repairs, exemplify our dedication to delivering exceptional customer service. Together, we ensure every customer leaves with a smile, embodying the essence of what makes Crew Carwash exceptional.
cons: The sole drawback I can identify in this role is the occasional monotony. There are days, particularly during rainy days, when the repetitiveness sets in, leading you to feel bored

title: My experience at Crew Carwash
pros: Working for Crew Carwash has a lot of benefits for their employees, from Health/Dental/Vision insurance, tuition assistance, free carwashes, to even matching 401k contributions! Crew does well to ensure that there is a healthy work-life balance for their employees as well, managers are expected to work 2 weekends out of the month, this typically means that you'd work every other weekend which is uncommon in the service industry where weekends are usually busier as people are out of work.
cons: The most common con to working at Crew carwash or any carwash for that matter is weather. In the winter it is cold and in the summer it gets hot. The team members are still expected to perform their duties in these conditions to a certain extent. If, however it is extremely hot or cold, you are not forced to stand out in those conditions if there are no cars on the lot!

title: Working at Crew Carwash

Being able to talk to one of the managers about anything even if not related to crew and they would be willing to help you and give you the best advices!

title: Love this job
pros: - the team chemistry at my location is unmatched! im really close with everyone, even management - managers are super flexible and have created an environment where i feel safe, comfortable, and cared for - we have contests to motivate us to perform well - iâm learning social skills as well as selling skills
cons: - at my location specifically, we have an associate who works full time so they always win awards and it sometimes discourages me, and iâm sure the rest of the team who work part time, from wanting to perform well for an award. it kind of makes my hard work seem unnoticed

title: Full of Opportunity
pros: -Phenomenal leadership -Opportunities to move up in the organization -Amazing team to work with -Great benefits with a company that cares -Service industry leading values and culture
cons: It does become a lifestyle! The higher in management the more you work, just like anything else. To make it that far you have to be passionate about the company and your position. So, for some that might be negative, but for a lot of us in the company that have made it this far, we take great pride in what we do and the company we work for.

title: Motivating Work Environment
pros: Working at Crew Carwash not only has assisted my socialization with a variety of people and create a comforting work environment with my team, but it has also motivated me to strive for more learning opportunities. As a team, thereâs always a form of engagement when working, that being setting your personal goals or trying to fulfill a customerâs needs. You get to use your creative abilities to ensure that people are receiving the best services possible which at the end helps you leave work feeling content with yourself. In addition, I have been able to comfortably manage working and going to college full time with the flexible scheduling.
cons: Working at Crew Carwash involves a lot of physical mobility that includes running, standing at all times and ,sometimes, carrying items which may not be suitable for people. You will be working outside throughout different weathers meaning during hot and freezing temperatures. Since Crew is a weather dependent job, working hours will vary greatly if the locations are not busy.

title: Onward and Upward

title: Great Place for Growth and Development
body: Great Place for Growth and Development
pros: Crew Carwash provides an excellent environment for both personal and professional growth. The company places a strong emphasis on training and development, ensuring that every employee has the opportunity to advance their career. The team culture is supportive, and management genuinely cares about employee well-being. The work environment is fast-paced but rewarding, with plenty of opportunities for recognition and advancement. Additionally, the company's commitment to customer satisfaction is evident, making it a great place to work for those who value high standards of service.
cons: The fast-paced nature of the job can be challenging at times, especially during peak hours. Compensation could be improved to better reflect the cost of living and the hard work employees put in. Additionally, while the company offers great support for career growth, more financial assistance for employees facing tough personal circumstances would be beneficial.

title: Excellent Work Place

Definitely a great place to work

title: Keaton Dolickâs Review
pros: Crew has a very positive work environment, with a team-oriented environment and very flexible scheduling for part-time employees that might be in high school or college. Working at Crew gives you performance growth opportunities, as well as employee benefits and performance incentives.
cons: You do run into a lot of customer service challenges at Crew, but our goal is create lifetime customers who love their experience every time they stop in at Crew. You may have inconsistent hours just because of our exposure to weather conditions like rain, snow, etc. These conditions keep customers from washing their cars and therefore some people must be sent home before completing their full shift. Overall, the pros heavily outweigh the cons for working at crew carwash.

title: Crew Is Awesome!
pros: Crew has a way of making their employees feel heard, cared for, and showing empathy. Working for crew for almost 2 years, I have built so many great relationships; Itâs not only a workplace for me. If it werenât for Crew wanting to carry out their mission, vision, and values the workplace for Crew locations wouldnât be as awesome as they are. I also live how the CEO, HR representatives, and many other positions try to connect with locations and employees. This is one of the prime examples of how Crew brings everyone together. I canât emphasize enough on how it all circles back to everyone being valued. Iâve never felt more human within a workplace before; everyone cares for one another and really values teamwork. Crew definitely set the bar high for when I get out into the workforce after college. No place like it!
cons: I will say that Crew does have cons because they arenât perfect, but not bad enough to list them. For example, something small would be like new tiles in the restroom. I truly love and appreciate Crew as a whole, so itâs hard to write in the âconsâ section; it seems like I am picking now. Crew is amazing!

title: Empowering Our Team: A Commitment to Excellence at Crew Carwash
body: Empowering Our Team: A Commitment to Excellence at Crew Carwash
pros: As part of the management team at Crew Carwash, I am proud to witness daily the incredible dedication and teamwork that define our workplace. We prioritize open communication and a supportive environment, which I believe are key to our success. Our team members enjoy flexible scheduling that accommodates their personal needs, fostering a healthy work-life balance. We also take great pride in our customer service, knowing that our collective effort brings satisfaction and loyalty among our customers.
cons: The job can be physically demanding, especially in the winter months, which is an inherent part of the carwash industry. We continually strive to ensure that all team members have the support they need to excel without compromising their health or well-being.

title: Positive and Rewarding Environment
pros: Crew culture is full of positivity from our Team Members at the locations to the entire Support Team. Opportunities to grow with the company are everywhere. Crew provides plenty of benefits such as tuition assistance and loan repayment which helped me get through school. They provide interactive classroom training which will not only help you develop in your career, but in your personal life as well. You are pushed to achieve your full potential.
cons: I really can't think of many cons!

title: Working at Crew Carwash has been a rewarding experience.

cons: One of the biggest downsides to working at crew is the weather. Freezing cold or blazing hot? Youâll feel it all. The uniforms are great to keep you warm when needed, but its not always perfect. Youâll be âexposed to the elementsâ here. But really thatâs the only huge downside I can think of.

title: Work/life balance and pay
pros: - You will feel like you have a second family, the people I have met here have become lifelong friends and I wouldn't change that experience for the whole world - If you want to be independent and learn a whole lot, this is the place for you as a manager, there is a lot that lands on your shoulders and I have always been given the ability to make decisions. - The ability to advance is amazing, they really want you to move up and if you have a good management team they will 100% support you the entire way.
cons: - They have a blackout period for PTO as a manager. Forget about going to see family for Christmas or taking a vacation over a weekend during our busy season, because it's not allowed. - No sick days, you have to use your own PTO for that, limited as it might be. And if your location is understaffed? Forget about taking PTO at all. - Expectations are extremely high for assistants and above, for very little pay. Moving from shift manager to assistant manager you don't even make 2 dollars more but you expected to know so much more and train like crazy to achieve that next level. It's almost insulting how little they pay you. They offer overtime for this position but I was already working overtime as a shift manager because of staffing so does it really make a difference? Not really, it just means that I don't spend any time with my friends or family outside of work.

title: A solid foundation for a first job.
body: A solid foundation for a first job.
pros: The depth of training provided to all associates is incredibly in-depth for what the job is, and given it is an early job for many young people I believe Crew does a good job at setting a foundation for many of its associates to go out and be successful in other areas. One of the greatest pros for myself is the variety and being outside. Even when the weather is extreme, I would still rather be outside than in a dark cubicle or under fluorescent lights for most of my day.
cons: There are two uniforms, and they both come from the employee's wages with no stipend. The summer uniform is fine, but the winter uniform is honestly quite expensive and required. Yesterday I had dirt from a car ruin one of my newer white shirts and tie that I will now need to be replaced at my expense despite it being ruined by doing my job well. There is a lot of pressure on metrics and numbers, sometimes taking the intuitive human connection out of interactions. I know I am very good at my job, but in ways that sales or percentages can't necessarily quantify. It is a business, and the business wants to make money, sometimes this can feel like it is at the expense of associates.

title: I am a sales associate in the WOW zone and load customers into the car wash
body: I am a sales associate in the WOW zone and load customers into the car wash
pros: This job is very flexible with my school schedule which is one of the main draws I had to the company. I am able to work part time at school while being a full-time student at Purdue University. It offers a tuition assistance program that helps me tremendously considering I am putting myself through school with no assistance from my family. While those are financial pros there are also many pros in the community of Crew itself. The team is so welcoming and makes sure that you feel as home and as a family. If you are having a bad day the team will never fail to cheer you up and you have the ability to do the same. It is a great way to make a loving community and find a place to fit right in.
cons: One of the only downsides that I can think of from working at Crew is the fact that sometimes the weather can take a toll on the mood of the shift. The colder and warmer shifts can be hard to get through, but Crew always makes sure that there are options to make sure everyone stays safe and happy in extreme weathers.

title: Glassdoor Review
pros: Great benefits, wonderful work environment, and honestly a good customer experience. Itâs a rarety that you get awful customers. I always have the opportunity to grow and become better at what I do and overall become a better person. Crew teaches you real life skills too.
cons: Although I donât really experience this as much with being part time and at school, sometimes when we are short staffed, you get scheduled several days in a row. It can just be tiring and can lead to burn out when youâre scheduled 10 days in a row. Thankfully, the managers work with you and try to get you out early and called off if they can when that happens.

The customers aren't always the kindest but that's to be expected in any retail position. Fortunately, the ones negative enough to remember are few and far between.

title: A Great Stepping Stone
pros: Crew is one of the few companies that offer a great tuition reimbursement that goes directly back to you. The connections you make with your team is outstanding! Everyone is concerned with helping to propel you into the best version of yourself! They know that a lot of team members who work for Crew are not in it as a career and have set up a lot of opportunities to help enable team members to still improve and climb the ladder. For example, a 16 year old high school student who is part-time can still become a team leader! They lowered the minimal hours required for shift managers so that college students could still be in management while going to school! Teamwork is one their core six values and they take all of them seriously!
cons: The main con is being out in all extreme weather conditions. From over 100 degrees to a blizzard in -30 degrees, as long as thereâs cars to be washed, then weâre outside loading them or selling to them!

Management and other higher ups are really easy to reach out to and they all genuinely care about each employee. Even day to day it's obvious that the employees are one of the most valuable parts of the company. Crew is also a company that loves to promote and hire from within, so if you're looking to move upward or make a career this is a perfect place.

title: Great Place to be!
pros: Crew Carwash is a company that genuinely cares for the people working here, and helps everyone reach their full potential. They do a great job creating an atmosphere that supports its' employees, and encourages professional AND personal growth. Advancement opportunities are available to everyone, as well as top notch training programs. It truly is more than just washing cars!
cons: -Working in the weather conditions, cold and hot! -Some longer hours and schedules for management.

title: Crew Carwash Review
pros: As someone who has grown in this company, I have gone through several training modules and workshops. The training is well thought out strategically and Crew really does everything they can to set you up for success to grow in the company. As someone who is also in school, Crew is very flexible with scheduling and hours to suit my lifestyle. I am able to stay full-time in school and work full-time as a manager! The pay is really good as well. Crew Carwash has allowed me the chance to step out of my comfort zone and grow in several aspects, such as customer service, leadership, training, and public speaking.
cons: The PTO days are very limited compared to other companies. The sick days are combined with your PTO days, which makes the PTO bank feel even smaller. There are certain periods throughout the year that you are not allowed to take planned PTO, such as vacations, as a manager. Sometimes as a manager you are required to move locations if the company needs you to, which isn't ideal because it could be a further drive.

title: Amazing place to work with room to grow
body: Amazing place to work with room to grow
pros: Crew as a company has a lot to offer. Whether youâre looking for part time full time or even manager there is room for you. It feels easy to find your place within the group and grow like a family with your team. On the business side of things. Crew pays well with whatever youâre looking to start out with. There is a lot of variety when it comes to the job. They are also very willing to accommodate for hours if you need to have days off. Growth is more than possible as well. Take it from me, a manager once an associate, they encourage growth in every possible way and as long as youâre willing thereâs not much you canât accomplish
cons: There are high expectations at Crew which can be seen as negative but as long as youâre willing to apply yourself and do your best there isnât any worry. The main part of the job is customer interaction which can be a positive or a negative depending on your viewpoint.

title: Great Company with Values
pros: The company seems to have high standards, but it overall improves your experience as an employee. Culture is very family oriented and inclusive.
cons: Standard pay isn't high, but incentive pay more than makes up for it.

title: A Family Business Built to Last
body: A Family Business Built to Last
pros: The biggest thing I love about Crew is the quality of the people you meet. Everyone I've met upholds similar values and are very family orientated. You form strong interpersonal relationships with your coworkers and have lots of FUN every shift. Working at Crew has greatly helped me become a more sociable individual. This job has helped me learn how to uphold an engaging conversation and to be conscious of other people's perceptions.
cons: You have to be prepared to work in difficult weather conditions and dress accordingly. Lots of hustling around to greet customers. Like most customer service jobs, you have to be ready to interact with all kinds of customers.

title: Great place to work!

title: Crew Carwash Review
pros: My favorite part about the job is who I get to work with. Everyone is so fun to be around and nice to talk to. We learn about each other each day and forms bonds. I also like the work ethic they try to help you build. They really want to make sure you enjoy your job.
cons: The only downside is the weather! It gets cold, but the team makes sure to rotate everyone so they can warm up.

title: Deliver what they promise
pros: The office and management really do make an effort to create a fun, safe, and energetic work environment. They do pay well, and they are invested in helping you be your best. Management is also flexible with scheduling you, so if you have an athletic schedule they are willing to see if they can work with that. Or if somethings sudden comes up, they will try and get you the day.
cons: - Just be prepared to get up early and stay late sometimes. - You will be asked to be outside in extreme temperatures. - Only closed for three holidays - There are a lot of little rules/requirements you will be asked to follow, such as things you have to carry with you all the time. - White shirts for a sometimes dirty job

cons: -In general, a very fast paced and intense working environment. Working outside in all weather conditions and quickly serving customers a majority of the day -Working with mostly a 16-22 year old age group requires a certain practice of patience and soft skills, your success is based off these team members -Unpredictability of schedule at times due to staffing.

body: Truly one of the best places to work!
pros: Crew Carwash is one of the best in the industry. With extremely flexible schedule for Students in both a management and associate position like myself. They allowed to take appropriate time off when needed. They offer many benefits from all from 401k, Dental and Vision, Tuition Assistance these are just a few to name. Most importantly you get a supportive, inclusive, and welcoming environment! This is my first job, and Iâve worked here for 3 years and I have been given many opportunities to grow and develop as a leader.
cons: Weather is always going to be a factor. From cold and freeze to hot and sunny. Weather is a big part of the company, so expected to be out in the cold or hot weather when signing up.

title: There is a reason it's a best place to work!
body: There is a reason it's a best place to work!
pros: The biggest strength of Crew Carwash is people development and the extremely rewarding environment. I'm 27, my house will be paid off in 2 months. I worked at Crew Carwash since high school. They developed me into probably the most successful leader in my family. I've been a GM for 4 years now and I still love my job! As leaders we help everyone achieve their personal goals, not just that at Crew. Crew offers great career opportunity and gives the tools needed for success outside the company. I don't know of any other company that cares like Crew does. If you care about what your doing, our leaders will recognize that and help you reach your full potential. Another great thing for new associates, our managers are In Production with YOU! We dont let you figure it out. We HELP! We are always there for our team members.
cons: The biggest opportunity that many employees. complain about is being outside in the weather. Please know, washing cars does require us to be outside. Please understand what that means before applying.

title: My Crew Experience.
pros: Crew Carwash surprised me in many ways, Here are some that mean the most. The team you get to work with cares for you and wants the best for you. The flexibility here is fantastic Your hard work gets recognized and appreciated. The benefits here are incredible too, Tuition assistance and loan payback for full timers, and many more available to you. The work environment is the most fun I've worked in, fast paced and friendly. If you are wanting to grow and learn the management team is there for you and wants to help you grow. You make incentives for selling passes, extra money to bring home. There's many more pros to go over I wish I could list them all. Here at the St. Cloud MN location we are right in the center of town near everything as well.
cons: If you prefer a climate controlled environment this is not the job for you, Outside it can get cold in winter and hot in summer. If you're a fan of staying dry, its inevitable that you will get wet with water here. This is a job where you are on your feet moving all day too, depending on who you ask that can be a con. Also depending on who you ask, this workplace can either be very fast paced or slow, it varies depending on many factors.

title: Apply to Crew today!

title: This is not a job for adults with children.
body: This is not a job for adults with children.
pros: It was a fun place to work! Your crew really did become your second family. Something new every day. A lot of hands-on training.
cons: If you have kids this is not the job for you! The schedule was a nightmare, you never had the same shifts and had to work every other weekend at minimum sometimes every weekend because for staffing issues. In my experience when you get certified from shift manager to assistant manager you received a $ .35 raise. I was told you get to work 42.5hrs now so you get overtime pay which I was already receiving from being short staffed so there wasn't any really adjustment in pay, yet you had to do months and months of training and go through so many hoops to make so little money.

title: A great place to work!
pros: Crew Carwash offers great training to help employees settle into their role without feeling overwhelmed. My team members are kind, helpful, and easy to work with. Management seems to be focused on growth and employee wellbeing, and they are willing to try new things to make that happen. It is an overall positive, collaborative environment and a nice place to work!
cons: The only con is my commute, but I don't mind the drive into the office because the Support Center provides a great work environment.

title: The Best Workplace!
pros: Crew Carwash does a phenomenal job with their values and atmosphere they make in the work place! Being able to show up at work and have fun and make connections with your coworkers and customers is something you canât find anywhere else. Also, Crew does a wonderful job helping financially with their hourly pay, plus extra incentives. I feel valued as an employee, and Crew is always excellent about making sure they hear our own thoughts and opinions on things they can do to improve and gain more satisfaction from customers and team members. Overall, I believe Crew is one-of-a-kind!
cons: It is really hard to find any cons with Crew! My only piece of advice is to continue gaining feedback from team members and making advancements in the workplace as they become available!

title: Working at Crew is awesome
pros: I started as an associate at one of the locations and in less than a year I became a Team Leader. After that I opened my check list to become a manager but decided to take a different route and moved into their Customer Support Department. They made the transition very simple and my team was always supportive. Everyone is so nice and caring. Im happy I am able to help the Spanish speaking customers from this department. The skills you learn in this job is amazing, teams are always supportive and their training is wonderful.
cons: I would love to see more bilingual people work in the Customer Support Department!

With the team I'm on, EVERYONE is such a team player and are the sweetest people I've ever worked with! Working with Crew you can better your education and/or work yourself up in the company. The benefits are great too!

A lot of room for growth and personal development and everyone does their part to help you achieve those goals!
cons: Some people find working in different weather extremes to be a con. Whether is is 100 degrees or -10 degrees we are out there with the customer! If you wear appropriate winter/summer clothing and rotate like we should, this becomes a manageable obstacle!

title: Working at Crew
pros: - great coworkers - flexible schedule (can be off a weekday) - great work environment - room for growth and the endless opportunities to learn EVERYTHING! - useful knowledge to help you grow as a manager and individual - tuition reimbursement and assistance
cons: - working weekends and holidays - long hours if someone calls off

title: Truly one of the best places to work!
body: Truly one of the best places to work!
pros: If you're looking for a fun, friendly, and inviting work place, Crew is the place for you! I have worked at many different locations over the past 5 years and no matter where I was, I was always welcomed. It starts with the great leadership and management. Without them, I don't think Crew would be the amazing work place it is now. The opportunities for growth are another plus here at Crew. I started as a customer service team member for 4 years, then made my way into the Recruiter role where I now get to recruit the best team members! Not only is there room to grow within the locations, but also into the support roles at our main corporate office. Flexible scheduling has been another pro to working here. As a high school student and/or college student, this is crucial to making sure work doesn't interfere with your education. Crew wants to make sure you are successful in your school and work life so they provide a great balance for this.
cons: There is a constant movement of management.

title: Fantastic Management
pros: Crew always hires great people, and then offers great training.
cons: The hours are long and the work can be hard but it is all worth it

title: 10/10 Would Recommend!
pros: Crew Carwash is a company that truly cares about their people. They provide such good benefits and even Education Assistance which is amazing! On top of that, they are dedicated to help you reach your fullest potential. There is so much room to grow with this company and I have gained an abundance of knowledge and skills that are relevant for work and home life.
cons: You have to be prepared to turn some customers away in order to follow all of our guidelines and keep our customers and their vehicles safe.

title: Crew Carwash cares about their people
body: Crew Carwash cares about their people
pros: Crew is a family-owned business, and you feel that family atmosphere each day as an employee. The executive team always puts their people first, in their decisions, actions and behaviors. This dynamic has created a workplace where we all want the best for our team members. It is a true "do anything for anyone" team environment. Crew also offers outstanding benefits, including tuition reimbursement, student loan repayment assistance, maternity and paternity leave, 401k match, just to name a few.
cons: From a corporate support perspective, one of the biggest challenges is helping maintain the consistent wash and experience from location to location as we grow. We've are growing at a rapid pace, including expansion into Minnesota. And being a service business, it can lead to to a few longer days. Mostly a normal schedule thought

title: Great company

cons: If you don't like high standards and don't have a strong work ethic, this isn't a good it

title: Great Workplace

cons: No cons come to mind.

title: A Great Family Company
pros: Crew Carwash is a great and growing family company. Meaning it is NOT too big that employees get lost in the shuffle but big enough for growth opportunities. Crew offers a great benefits package, ongoing/top notch training, excellent pay, and a fun working environment. Everyone is proud to be a part of making smiles and we all work very hard to be the best in the industry. Management works very hard to always lead with our mission, vision and values, everything they do has a solid footing in our values which makes for an amazing company culture.
cons: The commute. I live far from the support center/headquarter.

title: Difficult Job that provides excellent experience
body: Difficult Job that provides excellent experience
pros: The greatest benefit of working as a Help Desk Technician at Crew Carwash is the immense variety of different problems you encounter on a daily basis. This helps improve your critical thinking skills and gives you experience in multiple fields of expertise. You work in close proximity to the System Administrator and Field Technicians, who are essential in building a fleshed out and complete understanding of the technology used on-site at the Car Washes, and also behind the scenes.
cons: The Crew Carwash IT Department is small, and there is a large workload for each person. The time threshold required to keep our heads above water is barely met, even though we have to work mandatory overtime. The environment is very stressful, but this level of stress leads to personal growth and aids in the development of time management skills. This is certainly one of the more difficult Help Desk environments, but has plenty of opportunities to learn from your peers and prepare you to further your career in the industry.
role: Current employee - IT Help Desk Technician

title: Fun place to work with friendly and incredibly talented employees
body: Fun place to work with friendly and incredibly talented employees
pros: Great management with very clear expectations and paths for career development. Full benefits with tuition reimbursement and a fun work environment. Unique industry where you are taught lifelong skills. Crew is very flexible with your needs when life happens. As long as you are open, honest, and respectful with how you talk about how you feel you will be heard.
cons: Still a growing company so sometimes things change fast, which is also a pro for me.

title: Toxic Positivity and Old School Mindset
body: Toxic Positivity and Old School Mindset
pros: Friendly coworkers, company has a good reputation and is well known
cons: Toxic positivity - feels like you canât have a bad day because of âWITâ mindset and hustle culture. Senior leadership is lacking in forward thinking and innovation. Just because something has always been done in the past, or because another car wash is doing it, doesnât mean you need to too. Pay is not great, average at best. Associate level is is false advertisement as base pay most employees will make is much much lower. There is an extreme lack of diversity in the company as a whole, but especially in leadership. Senior leadership of the company is almost entirely white men, with no concrete plans to make advancements in the near or distant future. Very very conservative old school thinking overall.

title: Great Company for personal and Professional Development!
body: Great Company for personal and Professional Development!
pros: Honestly, I love working with Crew! Since I joined last year, they have helped me develop personally and professionally. I started out as a part time evening associate and have worked my way up to shift manager (working towards Assistant). Crew also offered great benefits for my wife and I once I went full time. While I was in school they offered tuition reimbursement which really helped relieve some stress.
cons: Genuinely, I can not thing of a con about working her. Crew is all about accountability and helping you grow through opportunities that come up.

title: Excellence is the Culture
pros: Crew Carwash creates a culture of excellence in every facet of what they do. Camaraderie and shared values make this company an exceptional place for young men and women seeking a first job, as well as for those seeking a long-term career.
cons: While there are few cons to working at Crew, it is a rigorous job, especially once you move into full-time status. Crew has done an excellent job of continuously raising associate wages these past few years, but management-level wages/salaries have not been raised with the same vigor. Additionally, full-time work at Crew will require early mornings and late nights, which may, at times, create a burdensome work-life balance.

title: Looks Great Until You Get Deeper
body: Looks Great Until You Get Deeper
pros: Good pay and good benefits. Crew is generally full of people who love their job and make coming to work very fun.
cons: Working out in the elements, as Crew does not close when it is snowing/raining/cold/hot. My main issue with them is they fired me without even telling me why. I woke up to a notification of an exit survey being presented. Here's a direct quote, keep in mind that I did not quit, I was apparently fired for no reason... "We're sorry to hear that you are leaving Crew. Thank you for taking the time to complete our brief 60 second exit survey." Honestly I was so shocked, but as I think about it, the owner and CEO Bill Dahm only cares about the money he makes and his own personal image. For the Indy locations, he often visits and just sits in his car and stalks different employees waiting for them to mess up and he will call the store and yell at the managers for it. Most Crew support staff do not care about the employees or even the customers that get damaged vehicles as a result of the wash.

title: Crew's Reputation is the Real Deal
body: Crew's Reputation is the Real Deal
pros: What you hear is what you get at Crew. The reputation of professionalism, friendliness, fairness & high quality that precedes Crew Carwash is truly the fiber of the company. I wasn't sure if all the 'hype' was going to be real when joining Crew (I have seen other companies where it's just words and fluff that hold no substance), but everyday I am happy to be proven wrong. Senior management values and appreciates contributors at every level of the company & shows the same level of respect to it's employees as it does to it's customers. I have never had such autonomy in my role, support from leadership as well as fair and equitable rewards for my contributions before coming to Crew. I can truly say that I am PROUD to work at Crew Carwash.
cons: Crew is growing rapidly and with that come challenges to keep up with the demands of growth. This provides an opportunity for Crew to really solidify every part of it's business so it can capitalize on this growth.

title: Crew Carwash

cons: The winters can be a little cold and summers can be very warm but the company has a great plan in place to keep us warm in the winter and cool in the summer!

title: Crew has allowed me to grow in ways I never thought would be possible for myself!

title: Crew helps people reach their full potential
body: Crew helps people reach their full potential
pros: I've worked at Crew for just over 5 years now. Every year that goes by, I feel that Crew gets better and better with how they treat their employees (which is impressive because it was already one of the best places to work). Crew is really selective with team members they hire. Because of this, I feel that everybody I work with is positive and easy to get along with. Crew also will invest in their employees, helping them improve both professionally and personally.
cons: On the wash side--it's realistic to move up in your career quickly. At the support center, certain positions don't have as much upward mobility as I would like to see.

title: Great Company!
pros: Crew Carwash is a great company to work for, they offer competitive pay with opportunities to earn more awards and incentives. It is a great place to learn new skills and grow on a personal level that will help you no matter where you end up in life.

title: When your here, your family
pros: Crew is great to both their customers and employees. They treat everyone like family and, with it being a family business, your truly feel like you are a part of the team. A lot of companies compromise on their values- that is one thing I respect so much about Crew. They also aren't afraid to try new things. They survey employees and want to hear how to improve. Working at Crew, you have a network of locations that you know are there for you no matter what city you are in (where there is a Crew). Customers can expect consistency and employees feel respected and valued. I can not say enough about this company. The opportunities they give their employees to reach their full potential is truly a promise- not just cute writing on a wall. They do what they say and have meaning behind everything they do. I am proud to say I work at Crew and I hope that this will help someone as they are on the job hunt!
cons: Crew, like every company, isn't for everyone. It is a very fast paced environment with high standards. If you do your job well and do it with a smile, you will go far!

title: Great opportunity to develop into a competent manager.
body: Great opportunity to develop into a competent manager.
pros: Crew pays very well Benefits are great Upper management listens and cares about their workers You are given the tools needed to succeed (training, equipment, help from corporate)
cons: The hours are inconsistent. This is not a 9-5 job, you may start work at 1pm or 5am. Mechanical knowledge is required, if you are not someone that enjoys fixing equipment and coming in early or staying late to do so, might not be for you. They will teach you how to work on equipment. Taking time off can be difficult if staffing is rough, and there is a blackout period where you cannot use PTO because it is the busiest part of the year. Advancement to mid tier management happens quickly, anything afterwards can be very inconsistent and difficult to obtain. Job can get very repetitive, but if youâre looking for consistency this is a pro.

title: Best Job Ever!
pros: The professionalism of this job is second to none, and no one is afraid to any job. The training program for any job is very in depth, and they really strive for you to understand rather than memorize. There is a standard always wanting to be met or overachieved with any task leaving no grey area with your work or the associates.
cons: Hiring is hard sometimes leaving you shorthanded for periods of time. Seems to be this way at every location so extra hours, and adjustments to your schedule can become regular during these times.

title: Fun environment to work in

title: Why I give Crew 5 Stars
pros: Our Team Member Promise sums up why I love working at Crew. "We will provide a safe, rewarding, and inclusive environment, where you are valued and treated with respect. We will engage and challenge you with clear expectations, continual training, and coaching, so you will reach your full potential. Crew takes this promise very seriously and lives up to it daily. Crew is a family own business that understands that a happy, well engaged team is essential to becoming the service industry leader.
cons: The only con for a manager at Crew is that PTO can not be taken from Thanksgiving to Christmas. Thankfully, that is only 4 weeks out of the whole year.

title: Overall Great Company
pros: I really like the people that I work with - both at my individual location and at our corporate office. Schedules are pretty flexible, especially for the retail industry and compensation is on par for associates and above for management. Just recently, I feel Crew handled (and still handling) the COVID pandemic with an employee first mindset while still making strides to keep everyone employed without layoffs.
cons: Crew has been slow to make changes/modernizations in terms of "acceptable" appearance (facial hair, tattoos, etc).

title: Blown Away!!!
pros: Positive atmosphere, hard-working crew, amazing attitudes, everyone is always willing to step up and get things done! Crew does a fantastic job with their interview process. They weed out all of the candidates who wouldn't fit the crew values and in doing so, we are able to have a cohesive and Rockstar team! This is honestly the best position I have ever held in the workforce and my quality of life has increased immensely because of it!
cons: I honestly don't have anything negative to say about Crew. The only thing that might turn potential candidates away is having to work in temperature extremes, but this has not bothered me one bit as Crew takes all necessary precautions to make sure their employees are prepared and cared for during those times.

title: A Family Owned Business That Cares!
body: A Family Owned Business That Cares!
pros: I have truly enjoyed being employed by Crew Carwash for close to 4 years now! Itâs a great environment to work in where âfunâ and âteamworkâ are highly encouraged! Iâve made some really great friends working here. I also love the flexibility of the schedule, it makes it easy for me to attend my daughters appointments for example without having to take a PTO day. I love the âfamily ownedâ aspect of it. I feel Crew truly cares for their people. The leaders in the company try their best to make the workplace a positive and enjoyable place to be. Weâve even been taken to Top Golf and Kings Island for company outings! Lastly- the training that we receive is top notch. Whether itâs Relational Intelligence workshops or a Dale Carnegie course, I feel privileged to work for a company that provides training courses like these.
cons: While the flexibility of the schedule is a plus- the inconsistency of the schedule might be a downfall for some. It is not a regular â9-5â job. We do have a busy season where you may have to come in early/stay late or maybe even pick up an extra shift. Managers are required to work certain shifts as part of their 5 days worked for the week which include at least 1 open shift and 1 closing shift.

title: Great Company

title: Wonderful Place to Work
pros: Genuinely, I really think Crew is a top notch employer. In my experience with this company I've felt better taken care of than with any previous employer. I have coworkers who care about my skills and training, and they coach me regularly to highlight what is going well and what could be tweaked. The company offers monthly incentives for all associates and they make sure that anyone who wants a break receives one. The training with Crew is as good as advertised. This truly has been the best training I've received at any job, and I've even conducted training and orientation for a couple previous employers. I'd say it's really hard to not feel fully prepared for this job. The Manager in Training program itself is phenomenal. The training dives into such depth that you really would be surprised that they care such about making sure you truly understand every aspect of running a carwash.
cons: You're on your feet all day, but that's to be expected. The job can feel a little stressful at times, but typically everyone pitches in together to help make things feel a bit easier.

The worst part of the job is the weather. The company makes you wear only their uniform and it is not warm enough for the winter. They have been there for me in some of my hardest times. -They push you to better yourself, and help you along the way.

title: Great people, good pay, active work
body: Great people, good pay, active work
pros: The people there are fun and easy to work with. The job stays busy, which helps the days go by faster. Scheduling is very flexible. They will work with you on about pretty much anything so long as you can still make time for a full shift. They consistently have rewards programs to inspire employees to do their best work.
cons: Weather is a large factor: you have to be ok with working outside and with water during weather extremes such as snow or heat waves. You are on your feet running around on concrete for your whole shift, so good shoes are a must. The work can sometimes get monotonous, but you at least get to rotate positions semi regularly.

title: Great Employer, Develop Skills, Slightly Demanding
body: Great Employer, Develop Skills, Slightly Demanding
pros: Overall, great experience. Developed skills as an entry level position. Monthly contests between teammates which resulted in bonuses. Free carwash every week. Discounts during holidays. Highly competitive interviews. Great place to advance!!! Managers make a lot.
cons: "time to lean, time to clean" mantra. Have to pay for uniforms. Stand in cold when freezing.

title: Best Company Iâve ever worked for!
body: Best Company Iâve ever worked for!
pros: If you enjoy working for a company with an amazing culture and people, Crew Carwash wins hands down! From the very top of leadership all the way down including office support/construction/maintenance/management/location team members Crew truly has the best people. Itâs has a great work environment and a job you can look forward to going to. They truly help you grow and reach your full potential. Training is top notch from learning from your peers and other management team members to training working shops at the office or other locations they set you up with the tools and skills to succeed in your role.
cons: It can be difficult when itâs extremely cold but you get used to it. Depending on staffing work hours can be difficult for managers (clopens being the biggest one) however it has changed a little bit with open shifts being 30 min later so itâs getting better.

title: Great Job, Better People
pros: The people Crew hires are beyond the best I've ever worked with. I've never felt like I've had a boss, just people there to help coach and guide you along. Hard work and a good attitude will take you further here than anywhere I've worked in the past. The pay is
cons: There really aren't any but things that are different would be: Working outside in all extremes. We are prepared for this and it is something you get used to pretty quickly. This is a more hands on managerial role than other businesses so be prepared for that. There are aspects of maintenance that are expected but you are fully trained on them. These aren't really cons, just differences from other similar positions.

title: Amazing People
pros: Of the many jobs Iâve held the people at Crew make it by far my favorite. Crewâs upper management does a great job truly listening to their people and trying to keep up an amazing environment.
cons: A rather irregular schedule that makes living spur of the moment difficult.

title: Amazing Company To Work For!
pros: - Crew's hiring process is one of the best I've seen from any business I have worked for. Manager's are trained extensively and no matter what location you work at or visit, you always know you will be working with awesome people who were the best candidates for the job! - Advancement is not only available but encouraged. I started as a Part-Time employee and was trained into Management within a year of service. I was guided and helped in any way I needed throughout my training and encouraged to reach my full potential with the company. - The work atmosphere is straight up FUN! There is never a dull day at Crew, and I always look forward to coming in to work every day!
cons: - The weather can sometimes be crazy, staying open during very rainy, extreme hot or cold days can become a little taxing. - Lots of promotions. Some customers do not appreciate the feeling of "being sold too" even if the promotions save them money; the same type of promotions over long periods of time can become monotonous to the customer.
