title: I work around the best group of individuals to makeup the best team around!
body: I work around the best group of individuals to makeup the best team around!
pros: Advancement within the company is unmatched. The people youâll work around will become way more than co-workers. The flexible hours are great for working around your schedule.
cons: Working in extreme weather conditions at times.

title: Organized, Rewarding yet challenging workplace
pros: - Very fun work environment - Great benefits/pay - Well organized and centralized training program - Effective utilization of associates - Rewarding workplace with many opportunities to shine - Upward mobility is encouraged, even expected - Excellent culture and even better values - Crewâs values are not just values, they are a lifestyle they are actively creating - everyone truly cares about each other and the customers - Flexible schedule for part-time employees - Strict about getting the hours you expect
cons: - The weather (extreme hots and extreme colds)

title: Crew Carwash
pros: Great job with lots if benefits
cons: Hard work and many people complain

title: Best work-place culture Iâve experienced
pros: You get to work with great people. There are opportunities for advancement. They listen to feedback and make changes. There is a great support system.
cons: Some weeks you will work over your required amount of hours. However, by the end of the year it usually evens out.

title: Great Company
pros: I work with some of the best people I have ever met. Whether it is an office employee, a maintenance team member, or an executive you will always work with someone friendly that enjoys their job. There is plenty of room to grow as the company is in growth mode with attainable long term goals.
cons: For the amount of labor we put in and the weather extremes we deal with I feel that the PTO time is severely lacking. It can really wear you out and cause days off to feel like catch up days for resting.

title: A company who cares
pros: Crew Carwash has provided such a great environment to work in. The type of people that you get to work with is truly amazing. From the upper management all the way down to the associates, you get to work with people who actually care about you. There are so many opportunities to progress in this company and there are so many great benefits, from the healthcare benefits, schooling assistance program, referral programs, wash passes, monthly awards and much more! Working here has given me experience that is useful for work but also outside of work as well.
cons: Crew Carwash is open 7 days a week so there are times when you do have to work on the weekends. There is a rotation for working the weekends though so we donât have to work every weekend. Lastly, you have to get used to working in extreme weather conditions such as the extreme heat of the summer and those frigid winter days.

title: Great place to work and grow!
body: Great place to work and grow!
pros: There are many pros at Crew. They help build your leadership qualities at all levels. It is great how they promote within and also have a fast track program for employees with management or leadership background.
cons: There are no cons that I can think of. Keep up the great work.

title: Fun and friendly work environment
pros: Crew is driven and lives by values that set most other businesses apart. Schedules are flexible. Although a successful and large, locally ran business, everyone works together as a small family business would, which provides a great feeling of cohesiveness among all stores and corporate departments. Fun and rewarding with great benefits. Those who work hard are acknowledged and given opportunity to advance.
cons: Not many downsides to working at Crew. Weather extremes would be the only bad part, however measures are taken to keep everyone safe and healthy during extreme hot and cold days.

title: Growth and Stability
pros: Great people, award winning training program. Above average wages
cons: Retailer with varied work schedule including nights and some weekends

title: Great Place To Work
pros: Schedule Flexibility Great work environment Great Training Great benefits Good Pay
cons: Must be willing to work early mornings, late evenings, and weekends.

title: Best Job I have ever had!
body: Best Job I have ever had!
pros: Crew has offered me an opportunity to grow my professional career through a lot of training, and certifications! The job is a lot of fun on top of the staff you work with are so supportive! I canât say anything more then just join us if you are looking to grow, learn, and have a blast!
cons: Nothing bad can I say about this place except that you get what you put in. They wonât force you to grow, and improve but they are great at guiding!

title: Amazing place to work
pros: You meet a great deal of amazing people when working for this company. They expect nothing but the best in all aspects of business. Awesome work environment.
cons: Work schedule can be hectic for people with families.

title: Come work for a great company!
body: Come work for a great company!
pros: The people Crew Carwash hires makes all the difference in a great work environment. Everyone who is hired has a great personality and it makes everyday fun! The leadership of this organization also has a great focus on what employees need to keep engaged and further the goals of the entire company.
cons: This just is part of the job but...We work in all weather extremes. Crew does have policies in place to help offset the really cold and the really hot so just be prepared! (Even this is not that bad!!)

title: Iâve been working for Crew Carwash for 2 years and 4 months. Started as a cleanup year later shift manager
body: Iâve been working for Crew Carwash for 2 years and 4 months. Started as a cleanup year later shift manager
pros: Great place to work. Amazing management and team members. Great benefits. Great hours Flexible schedules Tuition reimbursement. Great career path. Everyone is treated the same from associates to management everyone is on the same level every one does the same job and they treat you as a human not as another employee.
cons: I believe only bad part about working at crew will be working outdoors on the heat or cold but even that they still take care of you by providing necessary to protect you from the heat and cold we also do rotations to keep everyone safe.

title: Crew Carwash Fast Tracker Review
pros: The pay rate is very competitive, average 2 days off a week, holiday pay, good benefits, requesting time off is very easy, training programs at crew are top notch. Itâs one of the best companies Iâve ever had the privilege to work at.
cons: Working outside in the winter.

title: Great Company With A Solid Background And Bright Future
body: Great Company With A Solid Background And Bright Future
cons: The downsides of working at Crew Carwash are relevant to the service industry. Shifts can be shortened or lengthened based on the volume of cars being washed. This means that on a busy day you might be asked to stay for an extra hour or two, versus on a slower rainy day you might leave a shift an hour or two early, so it does kind of end up even in the end. Weather also plays a huge role in our business. Due to this, working in the extreme heat and extreme cold are necessary, but is not for everyone. However, Crew does it's best to keep everyone healthy and safe during extreme hot and cold temperatures by providing you with breaks and gear to keep you warm or cool depending on the season.

title: Fun Company, Endless Oppurtunities
pros: I have so many things I love about Crew! -Fun Environment -Great Benefits -Rewarding -Great Incentives -Training is a mix of hands on/classroom I left Crew and was gone for a couple of years and will be the first to tell anyone that sometimes you need to leave in order to truly appreciate what is in front of you. I was hired back in to the manager-in-training program and am so grateful for this opportunity in front of me. The culture here is great and as long as you bring your A-game all the time and your smile, you will do well here! I am looking forward to growing with this company!
cons: If you don't want to get wet, dirty, and sweaty this is not the job for you. You must be prepared to work in all elements but Crew does a great job of providing you with the things you need. My one and only "con" would be tattoo policy. Anyone that has tattoos knows that it is a part of having them, but I hope one day this can be something that is more accepted. wearing long sleeves in the middle of summer heat can get pretty toasty!

title: Family Run Business with Corporate Like Perks
body: Family Run Business with Corporate Like Perks
pros: Competitive benefits including tuition reimbursement and student loan repayment. Friendly team members, growing business means advancement opportunities, training program mirrors that of large corporations. The President truly cares about it's employees and is an exceptional leader.
cons: Management and most of the Support Team is required to work more than 40 hours a week. Exempt staff has to either clock in or complete excel time sheets which makes you feel like an hourly employee.

title: Great Place to Improve Yourself
pros: Best people I have ever worked with. Detailed and comprehensive training at all levels. Team centric environment.
cons: Small amount of PTO that you can't use in winter. Often difficult to get any time off. Schedule can often be all over the place and hectic. Benefits are just okay across the board.

title: I have been able to move up quick and learn some great things along the way as well.
body: I have been able to move up quick and learn some great things along the way as well.
pros: Family oriented and caring of others especially customers.
cons: Uniforms can get hot in the summer maybe some type of mesh polo would work.

title: Hard work but opportunities there
pros: Great people and team, reasonably flexible schedules, great benefits for full and part time, training and support second to none.
cons: First and foremost as others have said; temperatures in winter and summer months can be difficult, however precautions are taken to mitigate this somewhat. Not so much a con to myself but if you are uncomfortable getting dirty and messy this may not be the job for you.

title: Crew Carwash review
pros: Great benefits, friendly team, appealing environment, engaged business, flexible schedule
cons: Working in weather extremes. Appearance guidelines

title: Been with the company for 12 years
body: Been with the company for 12 years
pros: Great family owned company that treats you like a person, not just another employee. The company leadership is very smart and always makes decisions that are in the best interest of all Team Members. You get to work outside and not at a desk. Always opportunities to advance if you perform well and show some patience at times. This is not a "stepping stone job", but a career.
cons: Outside for ALL weather extremes. Busy days with high customer volume means you will have to work longer days some days to help maximize revenue, but it's worth it and slower days you might have a shorter day so it all evens out in the end.

title: Amazing place to work!
pros: Crew is the best place to work. Everyone is amazing and fun to work with. The environment that this company has been able to provide for employees and customers is really something to be apart of. The job is fun and challenges you to be the best which is great!
cons: Be ready to work hard and be held accountable. This place is so fun and has so many great benefits. Best decision I ever made was making the switch to Crew.

title: A great opportunity for all ages to gain work experience
body: A great opportunity for all ages to gain work experience
pros: Crew is a phenomenal place to gain both work experience and customer interaction experience. It is a company invested in its employees and its customers. It is a great place to gain experience and grow in management as well.
cons: While I really like that we all have uniforms to keep a professional look, we are allowed to wear hats with visors but not sunglasses. This would be something to keep in mind if you have sensitive eyes.

title: I have been working at Crew Carwash Full-Time and as a Manager for a little over a year.
body: I have been working at Crew Carwash Full-Time and as a Manager for a little over a year.
pros: I had worked in fundraising for 3 years before working at Crew and took a Full-Time position to earn a little extra money. Crew has met my needs from a professional and a personal standpoint. This company has shown that my passions of talking to customers face to face, working with my hands and solving problems, can be met.
cons: It is the nature of this business that a lot of people (high school and college students) leave, because they are trying to earn a little cash. Our part-time and even full-time associate jobs aren't a forever thing which creates turnover. Even though this is a con to the business, I would also say that Crew has a great hiring process in place to hire the best to give our customers the best service possible.

title: Fast paced and great benefits
pros: Crew is a great place to work. Great benefits and great starting pay. Iâm currently in the fast track program to become a manager. It is challenging to learn the whole business in a short period of time but I have a great team supporting me. Also very flexible with scheduling.
cons: The only downside I can even think of is being exposed to the elements. As long as you can deal with the heat and cold, this is barely a con.

title: Great company to work for.
pros: This has been the best company I have worked for. I was hired in as a manager in training. The process was very detailed on what was going to be expected of me when starting. I had to interview with multiple people to make sure I was the right fit. That interview process only means that your co workers and yourself live up to the standards that they expect. I couldn't of picked a better company to continue my journey of management.
cons: There are really no cons that I have experienced. It is a hot/cold job depending on the season but that is to be expected when being outside.

title: A Company That *Gets* It
pros: Iâve been with Crew for 6.5 months now and have completed their Fast-Track manager program. It is truly intense, but you will not find a company more in tune with the idea that âwell-selected and well-cared for employees will serve your interests infinitely better than the oppositeâ. Crew truly has taken its time to build a world-class training program, which, when coupled with top-notch benefits and competitive pay, truly equal an unparalleled employee experience. The training program isnât for everyone, but if you want to challenge yourself to learn all new skills and take a deep-dive into improving your business acumen, Crew may be in your wheelhouse!
cons: All the cons are really just warnings: be prepared to bring your âAâ game. Be prepared to work with amazing people. Be prepared to work hard and to be rewarded for it. Be prepared.

title: Hiring the Best
pros: Crew Carwash embodies the best customer experience and they do so through their employees. They think about their employees first and how they can create lifelong employees! Benefits, room for growth (promote within) and opportunity, plenty of hours, etc. Hands down best company Iâve worked for.
cons: Hiring process takes a couple weeks, but also well worth it in the long run for the company/management team at the locations. They hire only the best.

title: One of the BEST!
cons: I wouldn't say this is a con, but because of the growth and volume of the company, things are ALWAYS moving... and moving quickly! I've always been at my best when I am busy, so it works for me. If you are looking for a place where you leave each day feeling like you have put in a good day of work, Crew is the place for you!

title: Great place to work (if you are willing to put in the work)
body: Great place to work (if you are willing to put in the work)
pros: great benefits, fun atmosphere to work in, challenging learning experiences, good training programs, structure and organization within the whole company, and always room for promotions if you want them
cons: scheduling and hours aren't ideal, and tough hiring process but it weeds out people who don't fit with the company

title: A Good Job for a High-Schooler
body: A Good Job for a High-Schooler
pros: Fun co-workers, decent pay for high-school, and it's fun to talk to people
cons: If you are part-time, expect to be called off fairly often if you are deemed not as important; working outside; and always telling customers the same sale for months at a time.
role: Former employee - Part Time Sales Associate

title: Great Management Training Program
pros: -Amazing Training Program that balances on the job, classroom, and study. -Thorough expectations from the company and Senior Managers. -Great resources to help managers in unforeseen situations. Your never left on your own to âfigure it out and make it workâ. -Company actually uses its values, purpose, and vision statements and strives to meet them everyday.
cons: There really arenât any cons as of this point.

title: Great
pros: Great company to work for. Has great values!
cons: There are honestly no cons other than weather effects hours
role: Current employee - Part Time Sales Associate

title: It will push you to use your mind every single day.
body: It will push you to use your mind every single day.
pros: Every coworker becomes like family.
cons: Very strict on appearance, safety (which could be a pro as well), and you become like a robot saying the same thing to every customer when selling.

title: Clean, Fast, Friendly
pros: Working at Crew opened a world of opportunity for me. Working full time as a manager while in school enabled me to build my resume while also really enjoying my job. The people that work for Crew are the best, their unique hiring process does a great job of setting up the team for success. Personally my favorite thing about the company was how easy it was to talk to the higher ups in management. The President, Marketing Director, and other high authority figures were always around the locations and always listening for ways to improve. The lessons I learned from working at Crew set me apart when it came to interviewing for jobs in my career field. I highly recommend young adults looking for some structure in their life to consider working here!
cons: Staffing. As a manager if your store is low-staffed it is on you to either make up for that loss of production or train someone new to come in and help. If you don't do well in high-pace situations it may not be the job for you.

title: Good for students... no one else
body: Good for students... no one else
pros: -great coworkers -occasional bonuses in the form of amazon gift cards are nice -weather really is not that bad, I got used to it quickly -tuition reimbursement program
cons: -raises are rare without promotion to management -monthly (or sometimes twice a month) team meetings at 6am -youâll be constantly pushed to bring in more big sales -gets really stressful on busy day and managers will âforgetâ to send you home at your scheduled time -always crazy understaffed or overstaffed.. no medium

title: Great Company
pros: Crew is a great place for anyone to work. They are flexible and work with their employees to find what best suites them. It is easy to move up in the company and hard work is rewarded.
cons: The weather conditions can make it a challenging place to work if you do not do well in the extreme heat or cold.

title: Company and my career are growing exponentially together!
body: Company and my career are growing exponentially together!
pros: Transparency, Training, Teamwork, Flexible Schedule, Detailed Advancement Schedule, Pay leads and is above average in the industry, Leadership is tops in the Industry, Bill knows and acknowledges most of his employees....without them CREWâs AWESOME customer Service doesnât exist, exciting, fast paced, family atmosphere, fun, fun fun!
cons: If you are not going to smile, say thank you, wave, draw animals, put customers first, have fun, get wet, get hot, get cold, be on time, pickup trash, clean a bathroom, go home a little early or stay a little late.....DO NOT APPLY. Crew built their reputation as the service industry leader in customer service. If you canât provide this service.....move on.

title: A wonderful company that takes care of their team
body: A wonderful company that takes care of their team
pros: Crew allows opportunities to grow professionally and is always willing to go the extra mile for a hard worker. Crew provides great incentives to their employees by monthly contests, benefits, and company-wide recognitions. Crew has the best work environment with the best people. We really do hire the best! Crew management is always open to team member input and changes, if something else will work better. Flexible with those who are in school and a great tuition reimbursement program! Also provide a loan repayment incentive for full time team members.
cons: Working at Crew is difficult, but is also very rewarding. Come ready to work! At locations, hours are dependent on the weather, so you could work less one week than previous weeks. However, this allows for great flexibility. Shifts can be easily moved around with your team. Since Crew is a retail business, we are busy around major holidays so we have to be strict with PTO and time off to make sure the teams have enough people to run efficiently.

title: CREW
pros: Crew car wash is awesome bro
cons: You should work here now!

title: Shift Manager
pros: Great training program and classes for managers. Great entry level job for someone right out of college to gain some skills. The company mission and values taking care of customers and employees. Quick upward mobility if you want it. Crew gives back a lot to the community and is focused on philanthropy within the Indiana communities
cons: Very repetitive work that can lead to burnout Being constantly understaffed Working in extreme weather conditions Micromanagement from upper level management. Work and life balance can be tough with working late on weekends.

title: Great Starting Job
pros: Goal setting and the people you work with
cons: Sometimes can get really frustrating working in negative degree weather

title: Great company that takes care of their employees
body: Great company that takes care of their employees
pros: Great management Fun work environment Base pay is great Constant opportunities for bonuses and extra money
cons: Working conditions can be harsh but they try to keep you as comfortable as possible Sometimes fast track managers can disrupt team chemistry, but not always Some company wide policies get passed even though the response is overwhelmingly negative, such as not letting employees carry pocket knives anymore

title: Losing their touch
pros: Crew is a great place for younger people looking to gain experience in the working world. Crew provides top notch customer service training and is very flexible for the part time role. There are plenty of opportunities for growth and to earn a little extra cash through contests and monthly awards. Crew also does a great job weeding out most bad candidates for a job. You tend to work with very like-minded, energetic people who enjoy coming in to work everyday.

title: Great Company!!!
pros: I have been here for a while and I have always felt like a member of the Crew family. My opinions are heard, my life outside of work is taken into consideration and I feel VALUED here. I have NEVER been with a company that truly values their associates, management and support teams as much as Crew. I LOVE working here!
cons: Be prepared to actually WORK. No desk jobs here.

title: Great!
pros: Great place to work and super flexible!
cons: Can get pretty repetitive after a while

title: Goal-Oriented/Driven Company
pros: 1. Lots of advancement opportunities- Manager Trainee, Shift Manager, Assistant Manager, Operations Manager, Lead Operations Manager, General Manager 2. Great pay and bonuses for Managers/Manager Trainees make 40,000+ 3. Associates pay starts off much higher than most companies 4. The company really cares for their people. They take care of you whether you're a manager, associate, support team, or project team. They want every one of their members to reach their full potential. They make sure you are always happy working for them. 5. Tuition reimbursement for associates/managers in college. 6. Loan Repayment Program for graduates. ($1200 annually) 7. Moving up the chain quickly is something they do very well with the managers. You get training from all the managers whether that be the other shift managers or it could be the area director. 8. Always trying to find a place to build a new location and always improving current locations. 9. Job Security
cons: 1. Work hours per week might be a little longer than what you want sometimes but that comes with any job. You do what you got to do to succeed and grow. 2. If you don't like really hot weather or cold weather, this job probably isn't for you if you're planning on working at a location.

title: Crew Carwash
pros: I have never worked at a place that I loved so much. The people that I work with are amazing, and continuously keep me loving my job. Crew goes through quite an in-depth hiring process, but it is worth it in the end. The people that work at all Crew locations are the best people in the state. Also, I am so happy to work for a company that cares about my growth, and wants me to continue learning and utilizing my strengths. I cannot imagine working anywhere else, because I get to be outside all day and work with amazing people.
cons: If you cannot handle standing on your feet for longer than 6 hours, this probably is not the place for you. Or if you cannot handle being outside whether it is 90 degrees or 15 degreees for over an hour, then it is probably not the place for you.

title: Manager
pros: Positive Team Environment with Great Training
cons: Work Weekends and Days Leading Up to or After Holidays.

title: High Standards for Little Compensation
pros: Because of their higher standards, the employees are a lot better than most. You don't get the weird employees you would at say, a supermarket. The workplace atmosphere is also quite nice, usually pretty open and free. Management was lovely as well, usually always friendly and helpful whenever I needed them.

title: Car washer
pros: Plenty of fresh air and sunshine
cons: Cold in the winter and early spring

title: Great Work Place
pros: Crew has a great culture in the office and in the field. It is growing quickly and will be an industry leader for a very long time.
cons: Crew is a great place to be!

title: very rewarding
pros: great people! great management! great rewards with amazon gift cards with different sales and challanges
cons: no work life balance since such short staff

title: Worked here for 2 years
pros: Advancement Opportunities. Good Training Program
cons: Lot of non value added rules; felt like I was in kindergarten (like sock length and having to shave). Turnover and staffing was always a problem.

title: Best Job I've Ever Had
pros: Great environment. Great management team and employees. Very engaging work environment. Corporate listens to feedback and communicates effectively.
cons: Be willing to get your hands dirty. We maintain our own equipment and install new equipment.

title: Safety Coordinator
pros: Crew is a great family owned business that truly cares about their team members. They want to develop you to be the best you can be inside and outside of work. They do this by having an outstanding training program that develops the team member to each position they want to reach within the company. While learning skills for the next position they work in productivity and emotional intelligence workshops to help you outside of work as well. All team members are super friendly and welcoming. This creates a want and urge to do the best you can.
cons: It is a fast paced environment. I came into a position that had been vacant and had to learn from the ground up what they were doing on my own. ( this was for a management position. The associate route is nothing similar.)

title: Fantastic place to work!
pros: You can move up quick, and there is just a really great atmosphere with the whole team!
cons: As of right now I have no cons!

title: Excellent Culture, Great People, Even Greater Job.
body: Excellent Culture, Great People, Even Greater Job.

title: Amazing.
pros: The people, I look forward to coming to work and seeing the amazing people I get to call my co-workers. I also love the uniform.
cons: None; depends on personal preferences.

title: good
pros: great company to work for. a job where you become a family and enjoy going to work
cons: better up to date uniforms

title: Great place to work!
pros: The people are great. Everyone has an awesome work ethic and a desire to be a team player. The management training is top notch.
cons: Weather can be trying if not prepared but Crew will set you up for success with cold gear. Also have hand warmers and hot chocolate!

title: Manager
pros: Crew teaches a lot about positive work environment, organization, and even more important subjects such as mechanical skills and leadership that really help you for the rest of your life. If you put in atleast a year at Crew. It will look good on your resume to anyplace local. Many local business in the state of Indiana actually try to recruit Crew employees cause they know they are trained well in customer focus and many other aspects. Even some employers I've been told are "wow'd" when they see Crew on job history on a resume. It's for sure a great experience that can set you up in the future if you play your cards right. There's also a great opportunity to move up in the company. I cannot count how many associates I've seen move into a management position, and management move into maintenance, office support, etc.
cons: Performance is key but it can bite you in the rear if you don't stay on top. Regular performance appraisals and reviews are common. Mystery shopper reports with bad grades and lead to termination. The companies does a good job on their company promise but I have seen it fail multiple times. I've seen favoritism and fraternizing happen a lot even though the company claims to be strictly against it. I actually lost a promotion to someone who should have been fired for inappropriate manager associate relationships. But since they were the favorite, they ended up keeping their job and advancing at the same time. Which caused a lot of people to quit or get fired in the process.

title: There are good jobs out there.
body: There are good jobs out there.
pros: I work 9 hour days but they are the most stress free hours/weeks I've had since leaving previous employers.
cons: Not a downside but we handle vehicles that people pay a lot of money for. If we don't do our job, we hear about it. Very rare though.

title: Wash Associate
pros: Fun work learn new skills interesting place to work Taught me what hard work is Good customer service exposure Safe environment

title: Cares about employees
pros: Many benefits including biweekly free washes (works, ultimate), competitions between crew members for cash prizes and more that I canât think of at the moment. Great training for regular employees and managers. The managers at the location I work at are very proficient and have high accountability for their actions. Crew does not keep management staff who donât follow their vision ( a good thing).
cons: Nothing outstanding, the only thing I can think of is you need to stand all the time during your shift, thatâs about it.

title: Wash Associate
pros: The culture is what makes Crew great. Everyone there is hard working, and loves to have fun. The pay is great for the amount of work that has to be put in. Responsibility is there for the taking, as long as you show the initiative.
cons: No set schedule, some weeks are better than others for hours.

title: Shift Manager
pros: Great people, promote from within based on performance, great training program, Upper management will usually listen to your ideas and encourage them.
cons: You rely on overtime hours to get the full amount of pay they promise you. It wonât happen because you constantly donât get your hours because of slow days when they send you home early. Crazy hours that are never the same day to day. Uniforms cost you $30 a month.

title: Great Company
pros: -great team of people who are exited about what they do every day -working for a company that has a strong reputation in the Indy area -supervisors and managers really listen and care about your concerns and career goals -great training programs and courses -CEO, VP, and Owner of the company are very readily accessible if you ever need them and they truly care about their employees work-life balance -provided with 4-8 free carwashes a month -promote from within for supervisor and management positions
cons: -since it is in the retail and customer service space, everyday you will get yelled at by customers; you will need a thick skin to get through every day and to interact with angry customers

title: Advancement Opportunities
pros: I started out as a part-time associate at a location. With many advancement opportunities, I now work at the corporate office. The team is extremely nice and helpful.
cons: While working at a location, the weather can be brutal sometimes. Every team member is great to work with, so it makes it bearable.

title: Crew has helped me develop into a better leader and deliver excellent customer service.
body: Crew has helped me develop into a better leader and deliver excellent customer service.
pros: -Hands on training and continuous coaching. -Support staff is outstanding. -Flexible schedule. -Fun and safe work environment. -Outstanding benefits. -Team atmosphere. -Competitive pay.
cons: -Working in all types of weather conditions. -Required to work some holidays. -Strict uniform requirements. -Required to work some weekends.

title: Great Company
pros: Always listening and making improvements to the facilities and the employees working conditions. Believes in family time off and always promoting from within.
cons: Retail environment with crazy hours. 12pm - 10pm one day and the next day work 5am- 3pm. Long hours during peak volume seasons. Working outdoors.

title: Safety coordinator
pros: The owner seems to care about his compnay.
cons: They only care about bonuses not the true safety of their team.

title: Great company with great people that work there.
body: Great company with great people that work there.
pros: The location I work at creates a bond between the workers and makes you feel like the people you work with are like family.
cons: Can be a little repetitive doing the same thing over and over again.

title: Fast Track Manager!
pros: This company is amazing and is very family oriented. I love working around all of the employees because they only hire the best! This is nothing like working at a fast food restaurant where people are in and out all of the time. I could go on for days about the benefits of working here.
cons: There are absolutely no negative aspects of this company!

title: management team member
pros: Strong, family business culture that focuses on the customer and our team members High expectations with corresponding pay, recognition and rewards Fun team events and company outings
cons: Not a good fit for all - Best suited for high energy, happy people

title: General Manager
pros: -Excellent support from corporate team and peers -An abundance of young and energetic team members -Flexible Schedules -Freedom to give feedback and share ideas comfortably -Opportunity for growth based on work ethic and ambition -Competitve pay/Incentives -Being a part of a family owned business -Manager/Associate banquets -Off-site training that builds character both at work and at home -Opportunity to attend local events that better the community -Big supporter of local charities
cons: -Being in the service industry -Unforeseen troubles with staffing

title: Family Feel
pros: For a large company, Crew still has that family feel. They really care about our job satisfaction and genuinely listen to our ideas and feedback.
cons: You must like working outdoors, in all kind of weather.

title: Great opportunity!
pros: Crew is a company that truly cares about the teams at the locations and listens to concerns that are brought up even from the part time high school team members. There is a huge opportunity career advancement. They even provide training to help increase your effectiveness in being a leader.
cons: Crew is a little archaic in the guidelines pertaining to items such as: dress code and image. They do not allow any viable tattoos and restrict male team members from wearing earrings.

title: Fantastic Company
pros: I know going into work every day that I will be working with a great group of people. The training is very thorough and it is very easy to know what the expectations are. There is plenty of room for growth in this fast growing company. Higher ups listen to us at the location level and value our input which is very encouraging. The pay is pretty good and benefits are as well.
cons: Working in extreme weather conditions, both hot and cold, can make very long and hard shifts. PTO could be greatly improved. PTO does not roll over and there is a several month long blackout period which makes taking it a pain at times. Work/life balance can be pretty thrown off if someone misses a shift or quits as shifts are usually staffed pretty thin. Uniforms are a bit too strict and grooming standards are very old fashioned (i.e. hair length, color, no piercings for men, no tattoos, etc.)

title: General Manager at Crew Carwash
pros: I have been at Crew for 10 years now and really enjoy it. It is a family owned company which is awesome because you know who the owners are and they make themselves available if you need them. Everyone that works here cares about each other and we all work as a Team to succeed. As long as you are staffed properly you get every other weekend off which is rare in the service/retail industry. The pay is great...starts off at $10/hour for part timers and the managers are well taken care of too. I have never been afraid of loosing my job due to any type of downsizing so I feel very secure in my position and knowing there will alway be a job for me here.
cons: We work outside so extreme temperatures do affect you, but we have things in place to make them bearable.

title: At Crew Carwash I work with many team members who make everyday enjoyable.
body: At Crew Carwash I work with many team members who make everyday enjoyable.
pros: The best thing about Crew Carwash is the environment. Each location team is filled with great employees. The tuition reimbursement at Crew is also a plus!
cons: The challenges of working at Crew are the extreme temperates. There are many hot/cold days throughout the year. Crew does a very good job making these days enjoyable and safe for their employees.

title: Sales Associate/Director
pros: Flexible hours, fun work environment, big opportunities for advancement
cons: winters can be a little cold

title: Service Industry Leader
pros: They are flexible with a school schedule and allow requests for certain days off if staffing allows it. The pay is more than most would expect and the benefits are second to none. They offer endless amounts of training to not only better yourself but also allow for advancement within the company.
cons: Possibility of having sick days in addition to PTO days would be an added benefit. Also there is a lot of pressure to promote individuals into management and leadership positions even when they aren't exactly what we'd look for to fill those type of positions.

title: Assistant Manager
pros: Crew has a very fun and friendly environment. You get to work outdoors and have many opportunities to interact with customers.The team at the support center is always very friendly and helpful. Even the higher ups like the CEO and COO genuinely care about what is going on with the people who work for them.
cons: only about 13 days of PTO after two years of service and it slowly increases from there. no maternity/paternity leave or sick pay, so you have to tap in to what little PTO you have for those kinds of things

title: Huge growth opportunities
pros: Rapidly expanding company that securely holds onto the small, family-owned company culture. With a strong focus on manager retention, Crew offers benefits that are rarely matched in the service industry. You can also run your own location with ~2 years experience!
cons: You'll have to work the occasional weekend.

title: Shift manager
pros: Great work environment ! They really do everything to set you up for success. Many opportunities to grow.
cons: Long hours that take up most of your day

title: Operations Manager
pros: The team feels like family. Itâs competitive; in a great way.
cons: The unlimited program is taking away from person to person interaction.

title: Great Place
pros: Strict hiring standards means you work with great people.
cons: Harder to get time off during busy time.

title: PEOPLE -TOP TO BOTTOM
cons: If you are not a PEOPLE person, you might not fit in very well

title: Crew has developed my personality an extraordinary amount. This is the first company I've wanted to stay with
body: Crew has developed my personality an extraordinary amount. This is the first company I've wanted to stay with
pros: Fun people, good pay, flexible schedule, excellent training, potential for growth. They are investing in me like crazy. I recommend this Job like crazy
cons: Retail comes with it's own challenges. Crew also really expects each employee to preform exceptionally . Sometimes the retail hours are hard if you are really busy

title: Flexible and rewarding
pros: Tuition reimbursement, thorough hiring process, flexible schedule, thorough training.
cons: Working outside regardless of weather condition.

title: Fun and rewarding
pros: Crew has given me loads of opportunities I never expected to have. I started as a part-time high school employee 6 years ago and now act as a General Manager of a location. I love the people I work around, the job is fun and challenging, and the pay is competitive.
cons: There are lots of different aspects to the job, which is a pro and a con. You have to be able to adapt into learning all sorts of new things, that you may or may not enjoy.

title: General Manager
pros: -Great Training -Great Work Atmosphere -Great Team/People -Good pay -Company is open to feedback -Always improving
cons: -Working in weather extremes can be challenging -When short staffed puts lots of stress on location (however have a great recruiting team to help with this)

title: Operations Manager
pros: Great environment to work at! We only hire the best!
cons: Long hours, not much time to rest during shifts.

title: Great company, tons of benefits and great people to work with.
body: Great company, tons of benefits and great people to work with.
pros: Great benefits, great training and leadership, flexible and fair, very appreciative of associates and management. Purpose vision and values are truly what Crew strives to achieve.
cons: 401k contribution does not start immediately, you do not get a lot of personal/sick time during first year of employment.

title: The best in Indiana
pros: Great pay. Great culture. Great people. There is a lot of potential to grow here. Fun work environment.
cons: Long training process but it pays off

title: Service Industry Leader
pros: Crew truly puts in the effort to look out for its employees, and invests so much in making the employees the BEST part about Crew. It may start as "Just" a job, but easily becomes a career and much more.
cons: I have a hard time finding them

title: Assistant Manager/Fast-Tracker
cons: Staffing stores can be difficult due to Crew's very selective hiring process. Benefits are also somewhat expensive.

title: Great pay, OK management
pros: Great pay, high bonuses, good benefits
cons: Scheduling is horrendous, managers hardly ever follow through

title: Assistant Manager
pros: The people are great! This helps create a fun culture and atmosphere that really gets you excited about coming to work! It's a fast paced environment that keeps you on your toes. Upper management listens to ideas and puts them in practice which is great! There is really a connection between the management at stores and everyone at corporate!
cons: Long hours at times but this can be rewarding.

title: Assistant Manager
cons: Hours can be long at Crew, end it is difficult to staff the store since Crew is very picky about who it hires.

title: Great People! Great Place to Work!
body: Great People! Great Place to Work!
pros: The people, the culture and the positive reinforcement are amazing! Values that are meaningful and are lived by all. Everyone works hard, takes ownership and works as a team. I enjoy the team events and that the company gives back to the community. Feedback is requested often and acted upon. Feedback is given often and performance is rewarded.
cons: Sometimes expectations are too high for what can get done, but in most cases a little push back will help make proper adjustments.

title: People that care, a family feel
body: People that care, a family feel
pros: Very good training with good people that care and support each other. The President is great as is the Vice President.
cons: PTO season blackout and micro managed at times.

title: Assistant Manager
pros: This company is truly committed making all team members happy long term. Crew Carwash is a company that is going places and I'm sure glad I'm on board!
cons: Currently we are not allowed to show any visible tattoos. No matter what they might be

title: Passion for employees
pros: The people. Crew Carwash takes care of their employees, they give back to their employees, and they give back to the community. Crew does a great job of creating a fun and accepting work environment focused around serving others and respecting their needs. Tuition assistance is very helpful and their training has helped me in everyday life. If your looking for a great place to work, you have come to the right place. Crew does not just talk the talk, they walk the walk and that goes a long way in today's work environment.
cons: Growth can be a little hard at times, but I believe they are doing it well and really care about helping revamp older stores while at the same time building new ones.

title: Excellent Company to work for! A++
body: Excellent Company to work for! A++
pros: Great company to work for! Flexible schedule, excellent people, room for growth, and so much more. The people that work at Crew Carwash are what makes this company so great! Everyone works hard day and night. Free Carwashes are a great benefit as well!
cons: I don't know of any

title: Part-time Carwash Associate Review
pros: There always plenty of opportunities to work more hours. The managers were friendly and supportive for the most part.
cons: The conditions outside can be brutal, cleaning the tunnel with the dangerous chemicals can not be good for health.
role: Former employee - Part Time Carwash Associate

title: Great place to work with opportunities to move up!
body: Great place to work with opportunities to move up!
pros: Great people! The people make the job!
cons: Some long hours here and there but nothing too drastic.

title: Great company!
pros: Input is valued. Team members are empowered to keep customer happy. The company has great recognition programs. Best of all is the great people we get to work with.
cons: Retailer that operates 7 days/week

title: Great pay and benefits!
pros: People are great to work with and for, pay and benefits are outstanding, very flexible schedule!
cons: You have to work some weekends.

title: Office Support Review
pros: 1. Financially stable, growing company in a growth industry. 2. Crew prides itself on operational excellence. 3. Has a great reputation due to the employees they hire and due to their training programs.
cons: Heavy workload due to growth of company.

title: One of the best companies to work for in Indy
body: One of the best companies to work for in Indy
pros: Exciting opportunities for learning and personal growth. Fast passed, fun atmosphere that really makes you want to come to work each day.
cons: No Cons that I can think of.
role: Current employee - IT Help Desk Manager

title: Team Leader
pros: Great people to work with
cons: corporate is starting to lose touch

title: Asscoite
pros: Clean fast friendly. Best place to work at 16
cons: It is a very phisycal demanding job

title: Sales Associate
pros: Awesome people, great pay, good benefits.
cons: The weather was hard to work in.

title: It's a job
pros: Work alone and at night after close
cons: Body will not last long inhaling chemicals

title: Learn and Move On
pros: Great training program and culture. Worked with some truly great people over 5 years. Learned a lot about management and maintenance that helped transition me to the next step in my career. If you excel they will move you up the ladder as fast as you can climb.

title: Great company
pros: Tuition reimbursement. Emphasis on growing and devolving team members
cons: Sometimes things are made more complicated than they need to be due to them growing into a bigger company

title: Amazing!
pros: Lots of different experiences and training is constant and always a changing world for the better! Throughly enjoy it every day
cons: I honestly can't think of anything I don't like that is specific to this employer that you wouldn't see anywhere else.

title: Good First Job with Opportunity to Advance
body: Good First Job with Opportunity to Advance
pros: Good Starting Wage ($10/hr) There are clear expectations of you If you want to advance into management, the expectations are very clear and it is very possible to prove yourself.
cons: Very monotonous job Too strict on grooming, and what you must say to customers when selling to them. If you are not full time there is very little you can do to advance within the company.

title: Great
pros: Great people to work with.
cons: Being outside in the weather

title: Manager in training
pros: Advancement is good. Manager are caring and helpful. Job is fun. Good atmosphere . Most employees are good spirited. Manager skills are honed in
cons: Weather can be rough. Monotonous work. Associates can be troublesome. Pay could be better overall it is a great company

title: Great Job
pros: Friendly staff and management Competitive Pay
cons: Work Outside (Obviously) Physical Job Business varies greatly

title: Fun
pros: flexible schedule, bonuses, opportunity for advancement, contests, fun, great employees, always something new, team events, decent pay for some areas
cons: Uniform, strict, has to follow a script, too much training, too much job for not enough pay, late nights cleaning after hours

title: Assistant Manager
pros: Great benefits, good pay
cons: Not very flexible when it comes to getting days off as a manager, blackout dates on vacation

title: Good Place to Work!
pros: -Flexible hours -Good pay -Opportunities for raises -The senior management is wonderful -Many new locations being built
cons: -Younger, less professional managers (at my location) -Physically demanding -Working outside (Winter is the busiest season) -The uniform -Having to sell from a script

title: Rewarding First Time Job
pros: Sales Training, Communication Skills Growth, Team Environment, Sales Bonuses/Competitions
cons: There is a fair amount of pity rules that dictates your work experience on a day-to-day basis. Including clothing restrictions due to weather, amount of time based on weather, and that their is a set a script for sales which is a bit restricting.

title: Management
pros: It is fun and rewarding. Advancement is always there.
cons: Hours can be long. Early morning and late nights

title: Good development but taxing work
pros: The company always hires and develops employees extremely well. Training and coaching are put at forefront.
cons: Extreme working conditions. Working long hours with little rest in very hot and cold conditions.

title: Admin Assistant
pros: Great management & leadership as well as growth potential
cons: Not any that I can think of

title: Great Atmosphere
pros: This company does it right. They make sure to train extensively for each new employee, and the managers are awesome at creating a fun team environment.
cons: There really are no cons to this job except for the fact that sometimes you have to deal with a high schooler who slipped through the cracks and clearly doesn't want to be there.

title: A great job ruined by corporate ambitions
body: A great job ruined by corporate ambitions
pros: Fun, fast-paced environment Great merit-based sales rewards Promotions to management are very fair and straightforward Flexible hours
cons: My location had a 100% turnover rate in the year I worked there Understaffed constantly Heavy emphasis on sales performance As a sales associate, you must follow a very awkward, very narrow script with customers, that involves badgering them to upgrade, no matter what wash they chose. Failure to do so to a secret shopper results in a slap on the wrist. However, a second failed secret shopper in six months results in immediate termination, no exceptions. Tiring, this is both a manual labor and customer service job No job safety whatsoever

title: Disconnected office and horrible leadership
pros: Flexible hours, work with some interesting people, learn new things, tuition reimbursement for school, helps get real life experience on what you don't want from a job.
cons: Management barely makes $3 an hour more than hourly associates, managers are expected to work 60,70 hour weeks if the location isn't staffed, false promises are given to placate managers and squeeze every drop they can before they quit, unfair labor practices are common, corporate refuses to do anything with ineffective lead managers that have no idea what they're doing, no work life balance, favoritism runs amok, it's like being in high school all over again.

title: Great
pros: Salary, good benefits and fun place to work. College reimbursement was awesome and very helpful yo me.
cons: Outdoors in cold, which isn't fun. On your feet all day and that can be very tiring. Customers can be rude, as any would be.

title: Great place to work
pros: Flexible hours, great staring pay, management is good, coworkers are great to work with, free washes with paycheck
cons: Shifts can be pretty long, some management is not as good as others, some customers pretty picky and annoying

title: My experience as well as others who left.
body: My experience as well as others who left.
pros: Good training. Easy to move up. Hire from within. Good people. Enjoyable work environment. Flexible scheduling. Tuition reimbursement. Easy job.
cons: You can forget about weekends. "Fast track" managers are a terrible solution to the problem of lack of managers. Short staffed all the time and you will be stepped on and asked for more.

title: Working at Crew was a great opportunity!
body: Working at Crew was a great opportunity!
pros: The people I worked with at Crew were wonderful! Management truly cared about the employees, and about our life outside of work! The companies constant opportunity for contests and such were a huge plus in winning some extra money and motivating us to be good at our jobs!
cons: The winter specifically was so miserable. My fingers felt frozen many, many times.

title: Fast Track Manager
pros: Great pay right off that bat Depending on the location.. Great coworkers Good start for management experience
cons: A lot of things to learn to be promoted Can be stressful when you're on your own Depending on staffing you will most likely always work every weekend.
role: Current employee - Fast Track Manager (Shift Manager)

title: Director
pros: The people you work with can be really cool. Some of the incentives they give out like amazon gift cards are nice.
cons: almost everything about the job sucks! Long hours in Hot/Cold and if you are a Director most Service Advisors won't help you out with customers "special" requests which makes the job super annoying. They say flexible scheduling thats a lie. I asked to be taken off of opening shifts because I was working two jobs at once and sometimes wouldnt get home till after midnight so they said sure we will fix that. Well they gave me every single opening shift possible! Needless to say they lost me after that. All in all I wouldn't work there again and would highly reccomend that you don't either. Unless of course you like being micro managed and getting worked to death with very little pay! Oh and the "WoW" program is a joke. Basically working at Crew Carwash is indentured servitude!

title: It's rough but I love it
body: It's rough but I love it
pros: They hire based on personality, so everyone there is relatively likeminded and everyone gets along very well. They try to proved a fun environment. They increased the pay, so it now is awesome. There are a lot of advancement opportunities and management works to get you where you need to be. The tuition reimbursement is nice. It definitely helps take some of the pressure off of being in a 4 year college as well gives incentive to do well. It is very competitive. They don't take any crap. Though turnover is high, this helps keep a quality team.
cons: Weather conditions suck. Especially in the winter. Customers can sometime be crabby. With turnover being high, it still stinks to see some great people go. There are a lot of little gimics they add that can be irritating and unnecessary.

title: If you work with good people, you will love this job.
body: If you work with good people, you will love this job.
pros: Usually very fun/attractive people. Good pay They teach you valuable skills that will help you with future jobs
cons: If the weather is bad, your paycheck is bad. You have to stand outside in the cold The owners are psycho It is very strict and easy to get fired

title: Micro managed
pros: There isn't a pro here
cons: To many cons to name them All

title: Management Trainee
pros: Good Benefits, Training, in-class training, management listens sometimes...
cons: A lot depends on location. Some locations have horrible GMs who are impossible to connect with. Some are better, and move you along quite nicely. Bad Scheduling. tough to get a lot of time off

title: Terrible hours in extreme weather. Micro managed from top down to bottom level
body: Terrible hours in extreme weather. Micro managed from top down to bottom level
pros: Crew hires based on personality so you work with like minded positive people. Downfall is that management uses this to ask for longer work days and working with hazardous cleaning chemicals nightly knowing team members are afraid to disappoint them.
cons: Not safe! Slippery in the winter and dehydration in the summer. I've seen many pass out due to working too long in 100 degree weather using 100+ degree hot water to get bugs off. As said above the cleaning chemicals used nightly are hazardous and usually cause skin irritation.

title: Loved the Job but They Have Strict Policies
body: Loved the Job but They Have Strict Policies
pros: Crew only hires the best-Awesome Coworkers Awards & Recognition College Tuition Reimbursement If wanted can get lots of hours
cons: Lots of rules and policies Associates can't be friends with managers outside of work Easily can get fired-most employees don't last long Must recommend one extra service to every customer

title: bad
pros: Most of my co workers are very nice.
cons: THE PAY , petty managers, pay for your own uniform

title: Fun, challenging place to work
pros: pay, growth opportunity, competitive environment, good upper management, fast-paced environment, vision of the company, investment in it's employees.
cons: long hours, varying schedule, work-life balance, micro-management by upper management, too hard on younger employees, etc.

title: Great Part-Time Experience!
pros: -Well structured training programs -Fun work environment -Great People -Bonus incentives -Can quickly move up the ranks
cons: -Working in extreme weather (Hot/Cold) -Very strict uniform policy -Strict customer service models -Poor salary potential for managers
